concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains highly meaningful, full-page maps on pages 12 (Vicinity Exhibit), 13 (USGS Topographic Exhibit), and 14 (Site Exhibit). These critical maps are missing as actual image elements in the HTML output and have instead been replaced by text placeholders (e.g., `[Image: ...]`). Sighted users would be unable to view these maps.
concerns.output_quote: [Image: A vicinity map for the Cumberland County/Cliffdale Landfill displaying the Project Area outlined in a red polygon near Lowell Harris Road...]

concerns.description: Missing images: 36 of 56 source image(s) not present in output
concerns.analysis: image_count_source=56, html_img_count=20, image_count_ratio=0.5357142857142857. Over half of source images are missing from the output.

concerns.description: Link text pulled over but not hyperlinked
concerns.analysis: Checked for hyperlinks in the source PDF that were flattened into plain text in the HTML body. Found several instances where the clickable text in the PDF was not wrapped in an anchor tag inline. Specifically, on Page 20, the link 'Migratory Bird Permit | What We Do | U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service (fws.gov)' appears as a plain text paragraph. On Page 23, the text 'NOAA Fisheries' is an active hyperlink in the source PDF, but is converted as plain text inside a list item in the output. (The pipeli
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 20: 'Migratory Bird Permit | What We Do | U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service (fws.gov)' and Page 23: 'NOAA Fisheries'
concerns.output_quote: Page 20: {"type": "paragraph", "text": "Migratory Bird Permit | What We Do | U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service (fws.gov)"} and Page 23: {"text": "NOAA Fisheries, also known as the National Marine Fisheries Service (NMFS)..."}

concerns.description: Missing table(s): source PDF has tables not found in output on p12(source:2,output:1), p13(source:2,output:1), p14(source:2,output:1), p16(source:3,output:1), p17(source:3,output:1)
concerns.analysis: source_tables=16, output_tables=16. Pages with missing tables: p12(source:2,output:1), p13(source:2,output:1), p14(source:2,output:1), p16(source:3,output:1), p17(source:3,output:1)

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 2 table(s) with matching structure across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis:
split_table_count=2. Examples: pages 16-17: 4 cols (s&me, site photographs
cliffdale landfill
fayetteville, north carolina...); pages 23-24: 2 cols (name, status...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements. In HTML, these should be one continuous table to maintain accessibility and reading order.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 11 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=11. Examples: p1: "The text "Version 5/22/2023" displayed against a"; p16: "A wetland landscape featuring dark water with floa"; p16: "A wetland scene featuring dark, still water reflec". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 12 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=12, examples=['mailto:Raleigh@fws.gov', 'http://www.fws.gov/', 'mailto:Raleigh@fws.govorpleasecontact']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (40): Version 5/22/2023; Applicant; Page 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=40, pages_affected=19/29. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 1 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: (fws.gov
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=1. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Table cells contain 10 unlinked URL(s)/email(s) that should be hyperlinks
concerns.analysis: bare_urls_in_tables=10. Examples: p23: https://ecos.fws.gov/ecp/species/10515; p23: https://ecos.fws.gov/ecp/species/7614; p23: https://ecos.fws.gov/ecp/species/776. URLs and emails in table cells should be clickable links.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "S&ME"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1, no_th_tables=0. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 3 form(s) with 7 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=3, total_fields=7. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 20 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (18 truncated mid-sentence, 2 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p3: "... top left features abstract gray horizontal shapes"; p4: "...s the logo for S&ME, featuring a 2x2 grid. The top"; p5: "...n a two-by-two grid spelling "S&ME". The letters '"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=20 (truncated=18, placeholder=2, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Table row count mismatch: p14(source:3rows,output:6rows), p16(source:6rows,output:2rows), p17(source:6rows,output:2rows), p28(source:2rows,output:9rows), p28(source:2rows,output:5rows)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have >20% row count difference (delta >=3). Pages: p14(source:3rows,output:6rows), p16(source:6rows,output:2rows), p17(source:6rows,output:2rows), p28(source:2rows,output:9rows), p28(source:2rows,output:5rows)

concerns.description: Table column count mismatch: p5(source:9cols,output:3cols), p12(source:2cols,output:4cols), p13(source:2cols,output:5cols), p16(source:2cols,output:4cols), p17(source:2cols,output:4cols)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have different column counts. Pages: p5(source:9cols,output:3cols), p12(source:2cols,output:4cols), p13(source:2cols,output:5cols), p16(source:2cols,output:4cols), p17(source:2cols,output:4cols)

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "NOTE: If the proposed project intersects with a co". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.
